title: hypericum quadrangulum var. tetrapterum
intro: Hypericum quadrangulum var. tetrapterum, also known as St. John's Wort, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its four-angled stems and yellow flowers that bloom in summer. The medicinal properties of Hypericum quadrangulum var. tetrapterum lie in its ability to inhibit the reuptake of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that helps regulate mood, making it a popular natural remedy for mild to moderate depression. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of Hypericum quadrangulum var. tetrapterum can be found in various forms, including capsules, tinctures, and teas, and can be used to promote relaxation and improve overall well-being.
